qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: EmailSender.add tenant-isolation bypass authorizes spoofed local senders across customers
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is rooted in EmailSender.add sender-alias ownership validation in lib/Froxlor/Api/Commands/EmailSender.php, while the advisory is unsanitized DNS record content in DomainZones.add flowing through DnsEntry::__toString() into BIND zone-file writes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's DomainZones.add DNS-content validation flaw or the downstream BIND zone-file injection path.
evidence: The advisory describes unsanitized LOC/RP/SSHFP/TLSA content in lib/Froxlor/Api/Commands/DomainZones.php that is serialized by lib/Froxlor/Dns/DnsEntry.php and written by lib/Froxlor/Cron/Dns/Bind.php, enabling newline and $INCLUDE injection into BIND zone files.
The judge input manifest lists exactly one final qualifying finding: emailsender-cross-tenant-sender-spoofing.

That finding instead targets lib/Froxlor/Api/Commands/EmailSender.php and lib/configfiles/bookworm.xml, where sender-alias ownership is validated against the local part before '@', causing cross-tenant mail-sender authorization spoofing rather than DNS zone-file injection.
No final qualifying finding in this session describes DomainZones.add, DNS record content validation, newline injection, $INCLUDE directives, DnsEntry::__toString(), or Bind zone rebuild behavior.
